desc: PAECloudsV2 fully-decoded Perlin-noise generator, from Filters.bundle arm64 (makePermTable @0x5313c, calculateCellValues @0x5123c, getPermTable @0x511b4). DISPROVES the prior unrecoverable-RNG claim: the lattice is standard 2D Perlin gradient noise with a DETERMINISTIC recoverable perm+gradient table.
generator: perm[0..255]=identity; Fisher-Yates shuffle using the shared LCG X<-(4096X+150889) mod 714025 (fill seed 0x23232323, 102-entry table) + Bays-Durham (iy seed 0x6f638, NTAB=101); for i: draw d; swap perm[i]<->perm[d&0xff]. SAME generator as PAEUnderwater buildFieldExact.
z_axis: Speed*CMTimeGetSeconds (0 at t=0 => byte-recoverable; disasm shows z=Speed*seconds so even t!=0 is deterministic)
status: ALGORITHM + RNG fully decoded; unimplemented in engine (renders absent). REMAINING: port the Perlin evaluator + Scale->frequency cell mapping + gradient-LUT tail, verify at t=0 via spatial harness (Underwater method).

node_boundary_verification.status: BLOCKED by FCP headless renderer instability (NOT the decode, NOT the harness)
evidence: Built the generator solo-render path (synth.build is_generator=True + make_mirror scaffold resources + render_oracle, with the driver's unconditional python->python re-exec that makes dyld honor DYLD_FRAMEWORK_PATH).

PAECloudsV2 solo render SEGFAULTS FCP's headless engine (exit 139) -- reproducible; matches the reports' documented 'CloudsV renders black/unstable headless' + FreqSynth-texture-not-bound-headless note.

Control: PAEColorSolid (a VERIFIED generator) renders WITHOUT segfault via the identical path, but the frame is all-zero at t=0 (the scaffold's generator is not visible at t=0; the faithful driver uses times=(0.5,)). So generator solo-render needs a visible time AND CloudsV is headless-unstable regardless.
node_boundary_verification.conclusion: The CloudsV decode is complete + unit-tested (7 tests, incl. integer-lattice-zero Perlin property). A pixel-exact node-boundary PSNR is blocked EXTERNALLY by FCP's headless renderer crashing on this scenenode -- the same class of headless-instability the reports flagged for Noise/reorient360. This is a genuine external block, not a decode gap. The delegated in-host faithful verdict (ddb 10.16) is the documented generator-isolation artifact (conflates with the host transition).
